frameworks.name: KPI vs Metrics Hierarchy
frameworks.description: Strategic framework separating high-level business indicators from tactical performance metrics
frameworks.when_to_use: When building measurement systems or reporting structures for marketing campaigns
steps: Identify 3-5 strategic KPIs tied directly to revenue/business objectives
Map 8-12 supporting metrics that influence each KPI
Set up automated reporting with KPIs as primary dashboard focus
Use metrics for daily optimization, KPIs for strategic decisions
frameworks.example: KPI: Customer Acquisition Cost ($45). Supporting metrics: Email open rate (24%), landing page conversion (3.2%), lead-to-customer rate (12%)

frameworks.name: Marketing Funnel Attribution
frameworks.description: Three-stage measurement system tracking customer journey from awareness to conversion
frameworks.when_to_use: For comprehensive campaign performance analysis and budget allocation decisions
steps: Track awareness metrics: reach, impressions, brand search volume
Measure consideration: engagement rates, time on site, content downloads
Monitor decision: conversion rates, purchase value, customer lifetime value
Analyze bottlenecks between each stage for optimization opportunities
frameworks.example: Awareness: 100K impressions → Consideration: 5K website visits → Decision: 150 conversions (3% overall funnel conversion)

frameworks.name: Multi-Touch Attribution Modeling
frameworks.description: Credit assignment system for determining which touchpoints drive conversions across customer journey
frameworks.when_to_use: When customers interact with multiple channels before converting and you need accurate ROI by channel
steps: Map all customer touchpoints across channels (paid, organic, email, social)
Choose attribution model: first-touch, last-touch, linear, time-decay, or data-driven
Implement tracking across all channels with unified customer IDs
Analyze attribution data monthly to reallocate budget to highest-performing touchpoints
frameworks.example: Customer journey: Google Ad (first touch) → Email (nurture) → Social Media (engagement) → Direct (conversion). Time-decay model assigns 40% credit to direct, 30% to social, 20% to email, 10% to Google Ad

key_principles.explanation: Measure what marketing actually caused, not just what happened alongside it. 77% of marketers confuse correlation with causation in attribution
key_principles.application: Use holdout tests and control groups in content campaigns. Compare performance with/without specific marketing activities to prove incremental impact

key_principles.explanation: Balance strategic KPIs (lagging) with tactical metrics (leading) for early optimization signals
key_principles.application: Create content that addresses both: 'Our email open rates increased 15% this week' (leading) and 'resulting in 8% more qualified leads' (lagging)

key_principles.explanation: Mobile accounts for 60%+ of email opens and 55% of web traffic. Desktop-only analytics miss majority of user behavior
key_principles.application: Always include mobile-specific metrics in performance content. Highlight mobile conversion rates, mobile page speed, mobile engagement patterns

tactics.description: Group customers by acquisition date/channel to track long-term value patterns and identify highest-performing acquisition sources
tactics.expected_result: 15-25% improvement in customer lifetime value prediction accuracy and better budget allocation decisions

tactics.description: Track individual customer paths across all touchpoints to identify most common conversion sequences and optimize accordingly
tactics.expected_result: 20-30% increase in conversion rates by optimizing high-traffic customer journey paths

tactics.description: Set automated alerts for when key metrics drop below thresholds (CTR <2%, conversion rate <1%, etc.) for immediate optimization
tactics.expected_result: 50% faster response time to campaign issues and 10-15% reduction in wasted ad spend

benchmarks.industry_average: 23% of marketers confident in KPI tracking
benchmarks.source: Harvard Business School Digital Marketing Research

templates.name: Performance Report Template
templates.template: This [time period], [campaign/channel] generated [primary KPI result] with [supporting metric 1] and [supporting metric 2]. Key insight: [one actionable finding]. Next optimization: [specific action with expected impact].

templates.name: Attribution Analysis Template
templates.template: [Channel] contributed [percentage]% to [conversion goal], with [average touchpoints] interactions per customer. Top-performing sequence: [channel 1] → [channel 2] → [channel 3] (converts at [rate]%). Budget recommendation: [increase/decrease/maintain] by [amount]%.
templates.use_case: Multi-touch attribution reporting and budget allocation decisions

templates.name: Funnel Optimization Template
templates.template: Funnel analysis reveals [bottleneck stage] converting at [current rate]% vs industry average of [benchmark]%. Root cause: [specific issue]. Solution: [optimization tactic] with projected [improvement percentage]% lift in [timeframe].
templates.use_case: Conversion funnel analysis and optimization planning

common_mistakes: Tracking vanity metrics (likes, shares) without connecting to business outcomes
Using single-touch attribution when customers have multi-channel journeys
Making strategic decisions based on short-term data fluctuations
Ignoring mobile-specific analytics when mobile drives majority of traffic
Confusing correlation with causation in campaign performance analysis
Setting up too many KPIs (>7) which dilutes focus and decision-making
Not establishing baseline measurements before launching new campaigns
pro_tips: Use incrementality testing: Run campaigns in test markets vs control markets to prove true marketing impact
Implement server-side tracking for 95%+ data accuracy vs client-side tracking at 70-80%
Set up custom attribution windows based on your actual sales cycle length, not platform defaults
Create automated anomaly detection: Flag when metrics deviate >20% from historical patterns
Build predictive models using 90+ days of data to forecast campaign performance before launch
Use cohort analysis to identify which acquisition channels produce highest lifetime value customers
Implement cross-device tracking to capture full customer journey across mobile, desktop, and tablet
